name: Iñarbe Gorge and Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz
name_original: Foz de Iñarbe y Embalse de Itoiz – circular desde Aoiz
description: This difficult 40.1-mile road cycling route from Aoiz offers challenging climbs, panoramic views of Embalse de Itoiz, and the Foz de Iñarbe.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aoiz/Agoitz traverse a diverse landscape at the transition between the Central Pyrenees and a more continental climate. The region is characterized by undulating areas with cereal fields, dense groves of holm oaks, oaks, poplars, and Scots pines, and is watered by the Irati River. Road cyclists encounter varied terrain, from river valleys to the foothills of the Artxuba–Zarikieta mountain ranges, offering both challenging ascents and more moderate rides. The area provides a mix of mountainous and flatter sections, making it suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Aoiz/Agoitz The most popular road cycling route is Iñarbe Gorge and Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz, a 40.1 miles (64.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbs and panoramic views of the Itoiz Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ibañeta Pass – Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port loop from Aoiz / Agoitz, a difficult 80.9 miles (130.2 km) path. This demanding route includes substantial elevation gain as it leads towards the Ibañeta Pass. Local road cyclists also love the Roncesvalles – Ibañeta Pass loop from Aoiz / Agoitz, a 60.0 miles (96.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain towards Roncesvalles and Ibañeta Pass, often completed in about 5 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Aoiz/Agoitz is defined by river valleys, forested hills, and mountainous ascents towards passes like Ibañeta. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Aoiz/Agoitz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 1200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aoiz/Agoitz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: Road cycling around Aoiz/Agoitz offers a diverse landscape, transitioning between the Central Pyrenees and a more continental climate. You'll encounter undulating areas with cereal fields, dense groves of holm oaks, oaks, poplars, and Scots pines, all watered by the Irati River. Routes feature varied terrain, from river valleys to the foothills of the Artxuba–Zarikieta mountain ranges, providing both challenging ascents and more moderate rides.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: There are 9 road cycling routes available around Aoiz/Agoitz on komoot, catering to various skill levels. These include 4 moderate routes and 5 difficult routes, ensuring options for different preferences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced riders in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the immediate area, there are 4 moderate road cycling routes available. These routes offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility for those with some cycling experience, allowing you to enjoy the region's beauty without extreme difficulty.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes around Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibañeta Pass – Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port loop from Aoiz / Agoitz&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 80.9-mile (130.2 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31880199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roncesvalles – Ibañeta Pass loop from Aoiz / Agoitz&lt;/a&gt;, covering 60.0 miles (96.5 km) through mountainous terrain.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Irati River, which defines much of the region's character. You can also expect panoramic vistas of the Itoiz Reservoir, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986494855/inarbe-gorge-and-itoiz-reservoir-loop-from-aoiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iñarbe Gorge and Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz&lt;/a&gt;. The area is surrounded by the Artxuba–Zarikieta mountain ranges, and you might catch glimpses of notable peaks like Peña de Izaga in the distance.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Aoiz/Agoitz are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986494855/inarbe-gorge-and-itoiz-reservoir-loop-from-aoiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iñarbe Gorge and Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz&lt;/a&gt;, and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibañeta Pass – Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port loop from Aoiz / Agoitz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: Given its location as a transition zone between the Pyrenees and a continental climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling. Summers can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, particularly towards the Ibañeta Pass.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to challenging mountain ascents, and the stunning natural beauty of the region, including views of the Irati River and Itoiz Reservoir.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: While the listed routes are classified as moderate to difficult, the region's diverse landscape means there are likely quieter roads or sections suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with some elevation. For very young children, dedicated bike paths might be limited, but the general area offers a mix of terrains.

Q: What are the typical durations for road cycling routes in the Aoiz/Agoitz area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986494855/inarbe-gorge-and-itoiz-reservoir-loop-from-aoiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iñarbe Gorge and Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 24 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibañeta Pass – Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port loop from Aoiz / Agoitz&lt;/a&gt; can take over 7 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any specific passes or viewpoints to look out for on road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes lead to significant passes and offer memorable views. The Ibañeta Pass is a prominent feature on multiple challenging routes, providing substantial elevation gain and rewarding panoramas. While not directly on all routes, the Peña de Izaga, a mountainous fortress, offers spectacular panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.
tips: It is a very pleasant straight of slides... by car... because by bike you notice them in your legs.

Crossed by the Irati River, which has shaped its imposing canyons, this marvelous nature reserve offers a majestic landscape from any point you look at it. With the Lakorri peak (1,005 masl) to the west, and the Arbútxu (1,167 masl) to the east, this place is unquestionably postcard-worthy.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route, covering 64.5 kilometers with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 3 hours and 24 minutes to complete. This duration is for cyclists with very good fitness.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and a 'Hard road ride,' demanding very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain and distance. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride for road cyclists. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. A robust road bike or gravel bike might offer more comfort for these mixed-surface sections.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists can expect spectacular panoramic views, especially around the Embalse de Itoiz. The route also passes through lush forests and skirts steep cliffs. Don't miss the dramatic limestone formations of Foz de Iñarbe and other viewpoints offering stunning Panoramas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Aoiz. You can typically find parking within the town of Aoiz to begin your ride. Specific trailhead parking information is not detailed, but starting from the town center is a common approach for circular routes.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is a cycling path, bringing dogs along for a bike ride can be challenging, especially given the route's difficulty and potential mixed terrain. If you plan to cycle with a dog, ensure it is well-trained, can keep pace, and is accustomed to road cycling conditions. Always keep dogs on a leash where required and be mindful of other cyclists and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The pre-Pyrenees region offers varied weather. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter conditions. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, especially for rain or strong winds.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or considerations for road cyclists on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, while mostly paved, some segments of this route may be unpaved and difficult for a standard road bike. Be prepared for mixed surfaces, which might require dismounting or a more robust bike type like a gravel bike for optimal comfort and navigation. The significant elevation gain also presents a considerable challenge.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other notable trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You will encounter sections of the Roman road of the Pyrenees, Aurizberri - Esnotz ibilbidea, Garralda-Kadea-Garralda, and Berraguko bidea, among others, for short stretches.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes and a pump), appropriate cycling attire for varying weather, and a helmet. Due to potential unpaved sections, consider tires suitable for mixed terrain if possible.
